reasoning: `implies_apply` is external_body bridging `p.implies(q).satisfied_by(ex)` — a `TempPred::new` closure-wrapped implication — to modus ponens. Unverified trust assumption.
`false_pred() = not(true_pred())` unfolds through `not`/`true_pred`/`lift_state` to `!true == false`. SMT resolves this directly — no trust gap, just a correct property.

If `p.and(r) == false_pred()` holds intensionally, then `p.and(r).satisfied_by(ex) == false_pred().satisfied_by(ex) == false` follows from substitution. This is a correct consequence of intensional equality — no new trust surface.

Ex falso quodlibet: `false_pred` is never satisfied, so `false_pred.leads_to(q)` holds vacuously. The intensional equality precondition is the only non-trivial part, and it's assumed. No new trust surface.
